content_en:
When Tastar came under the total control of the Mujahideen, Yazdagard prepared his famous general Mardan Shah for combat and placed a huge army under his command.

When ‘Umar Farooq 4 heard that the Iranians had made elaborate preparations and a huge army had been mobilised to face the Muslims, he was worried. Finally, after a great deal of thought, he deputised ‘Ali +t to take charge of the Caliphate and himself set out at the head of the Muslim forces. But because of the timely intervention and advice of ‘Abdur-Rahman bin ‘Auf <, he gave up this idea and placed the army under the command of the experienced general Nu’man bin Magqran and sent him to face the Iranians. Since this was going to be a difficult encounter he sent Jareer ¢ to help him. By the Grace of Allah and the united efforts of these two great generals the Muslims triumphed.

During the caliphate of ‘Uthman #% Jareer was nominated to the post of Governor of Hamdan.

When ‘Ali % became Caliph, he sent Jareer bin’ Abdullah bin Jabir # to Damascus as a_ representative of the Caliphate at Al-Madinah. He was to negotiate with Amir Mu‘awiyah .& and persuade him to swear allegiance to him. But the talks did not produce any results; after discussions with his comrades, Amir Mu’awiyah # refused to accept ‘Ali 4 as the new Caliph. Jareer 2, while informing ‘Ali < of the refusal of Amir Mu’awiyah #: to accept him, pointed out to him that he had made arrangements for military defence as well. Some of the comrades expressed displeasure at this. When Jareer bin ‘Abdullah 2 saw this attitude, he was deeply upset and grieved. He thought it best not to get involved and moved to Kufah with his family. He spent the rest of his life in quiet retirement at Qargeesiya and did not participate in the battles of Jamal and Safain.

In 54 Hijrah he passed away at his place of residence and went to Paradise, at peace with himself. A hundred Ahadith are connected with him. Of these eight are unanimously agreed upon by eminent scholars.

The outward beauty and inner beauty of character of Jareer # were in complete harmony and accord. When his hair turned gray he took to dying it with henna, and looked even more impressive. All who saw him envied him his imposing personality.

“Come back to your Lord, well-pleased (yourself) and wellpleasing (unto Him)!” (89:28)

Ja‘far bin Abi Talib Once Jibreel $8) said to the Prophet &

“After he was martyred Allah 3 gave him two wings in the place of his arms which had been cut off; he can fly wherever he pleases in the

company of angels. ”

Ja‘far bin Abi Talib 2

He was the first cousin of the Prophet # and the brother of ‘Ali 4. He was ten years older than him. He was always there to help the poor, the orphans and those in need. He was a distinguished and true Muslim, who was the very image of generosity and munificence. He also had _ the distinction of migrating twice, once to Ethiopia and once to Al-Madinah. When the Muslims migrated to Ethiopia, the Prophet % appointed him leader of the caravan.

He was the Mujahid who enjoyed flying with the angels in Paradise with the two wings he was given by Allah %& in the place of the arms he had lost in Jihad.

His recitation of the Noble Qur’an was so melodious that it stirred the souls of those who heard him. He was a popular orator whose eloquence and mastery over the Arabic language and his daring rebuttals and prompt ripostes in debate stunned even as skilled and famous a politician as ‘Amr bin ‘Aas.

The Prophet # told Ja’far bin Abi Talib .g that he resembled him in appearance and character, and he was overjoyed to hear this. The Prophet # had also given him the titles of ‘Winged Being’, ‘TwoWings’, and ‘Father of the Poor.’ He had also elevated his status by kissing his forehead. When the Prophet 3 heard of his martyrdom, he was grieved and tears rolled down his cheeks. It was then that Jibreel sez gave the news to the Prophet 3 of the joyful flights of Ja’far bin Abi Talib with the angels in Paradise. When the nightingale of the court of the Prophet %, Hassan bin Thabit .%, heard of his martyrdom he expressed his love for the family of the Prophet # by extemporaneously reciting a poem in his honour.

@ Abu Bakr Siddiq

@ ‘Ali bin Abi Talib &

@ Zaid bin Harithah ‘Uthman bin ‘Affan 4

Zubair bin Awam ‘Abdur-Rahman bin ‘Auf 4 Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas

Talhah bin ‘Ubaidullah Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah Abu Salamah

Argam bin Abi Arqam - ‘Uthman bin Madh’oon bin Habib 4 Qadamah bin Madh’oon ‘Abdullah bin Madh’‘oon #. ‘Ubaidah bin Harith #

Sa’eed bin Zaid te

Fatimah bint Khattab us 41 +, Asma’ bint Abi Bakr ys a) 2, Khabab bin Aratt -é

@ ‘Umair bin Abi Wagas

@ ‘Abdullah bin Mas’ood @ Mas‘ood Qari #

@ Saleet bin ‘Amr

@ ‘Ayash bin Abi Rabee’ah 4 @ Asma’ bint Salamah Ys 5! 2) @ Khanees bin Hathafah

@ ‘Amer bin Rabee‘ah

@ ‘Abdulla bin Jahash

@ Abu Ahmad bin Jahash #

And after them Ja’far bin Abi Talib # entered the fold of Islam. He was distinguished in three ways. He was a member of the Prophet's family. He was a member of that distinguished band of people who accepted [slam first. And he sacrificed his life for Allah 9%. There is a tradition attributed to ‘Abdullah bin Ja’far, Abu Musa Ash’ari & and ‘Amr bin ‘Aas that the Prophet # used to call Ja’far bin Abi Talib ‘Abu Al-Masakeen’, (Father of the Poor).

There were four brothers in the family and there was a difference of ten years between each of them. Talib bin Abi Talib was the eldest. The next was ‘Ageel, then there was Ja’far 4. And the youngest was ‘Ali bin Abi Talib #. Abu Huraira quotes the Prophet 3 as saying that he saw Ja’far bin Abi Talib -¢ flying in Paradise with the angels. He also says:

“Whenever we felt very hungry Ja‘far 4% would take us home and serve us anything that he happened to have at home. He delighted in feeding the poor and the destitute and serving them.”

“Jafar te was a member of his family and resembled him in appearance and character.”

‘Abdullah bin Mas‘ood .% and Umm-ul-Mo'ntineen Umm Salamah ,,2, + 4! narrate that the second caravan migrating to Ethiopia consisted of eighty three people. Among these oppressed emigrants were such

notable Companions as Ja’far bin Abi Talib %, Abu Musa Ash‘ari <, ‘Abdullah bin Arfah 4 and ‘Uthm4n bin Madh’oon bin Habib

Umm Salamah 4—+ 4 2, writes that when the Quraish came to know that this band of migrants were leading peaceful and serene lives in Ethiopia, they were furious. Plotting to inflame the Ethiopian emperor Najashi against these Muhdjireen and have them thrown out, they sent their craftiest statesman, ‘Amr bin ‘Aas and ‘Abdullah bin Abi Rabee‘ah with plenty of expensive gifts to the court of Emperor Najashi. They were also instructed to meet the courtiers, ministers, noblemen and religious leaders, and give them gifts in order to bring them around to their point of view. These two men did as they had been instructed. When they felt confident that the preliminaries had been smoothed out and well prepared, and the important personages of the kingdom would help them, they decided to put in an appearance at the court.

One fine day feeling very smug and sure that their objective was well within reach, they went to meet Emperor Najashi. Najashi was seated on his throne in full regal splendour and all the nobles were assembled in the court. ‘Amr bin ‘Aas and ‘Abdullah bin Abi Rabee’ah wished him by prostrating on the floor in from of him. Pleased with them he asked them to sit on either side of him. Very humbly they said:

“Your Highness our people have sent these gifts for you, kindly

honour us by accepting them.”

“Emperor Najashi nodded and the gifts were accepted. Taking this to be an opportune moment, they again bowed low and said, “We have great regard for you; in fact because of our deep love for you and your country, we are taking the liberty of warning you that a few trouble makers from our country have taken refuge in your peaceful land. They have turned traitors to the religion of their forefathers and have fled our land and come here. It is because of their subversion that today brother has turned against brother and there are rifts and differences between members of the same family. Our sincere advice is that
content_id:
Ketika Tastar sepenuhnya berada di bawah kendali para Mujahidin, Yazdagard mempersiapkan jenderal terkenalnya Mardan Shah untuk bertempur dan menempatkan pasukan besar di bawah komandonya.

Ketika 'Umar Farooq mendengar bahwa bangsa Iran telah melakukan persiapan besar-besaran dan pasukan besar telah dimobilisasi untuk menghadapi kaum Muslimin, beliau merasa khawatir. Akhirnya, setelah banyak pertimbangan, beliau menunjuk 'Ali untuk menggantikan beliau memimpin Kekhalifahan dan beliau sendiri berangkat memimpin pasukan Muslim. Namun karena intervensi dan nasihat tepat waktu dari 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf, beliau mengurungkan niat tersebut dan menempatkan pasukan di bawah komando jenderal berpengalaman Nu'man bin Muqran serta mengirimnya menghadapi bangsa Iran. Karena ini akan menjadi pertempuran yang sulit, beliau mengirim Jareer untuk membantunya. Dengan karunia Allah dan upaya bersatu dari dua jenderal besar ini, kaum Muslimin meraih kemenangan.

Selama masa kekhalifahan 'Uthman, Jareer ditunjuk sebagai Gubernur Hamdan.

Ketika 'Ali menjadi Khalifah, beliau mengirim Jareer bin 'Abdullah bin Jabir ke Damaskus sebagai perwakilan Kekhalifahan di Al-Madinah. Ia ditugaskan bernegosiasi dengan Amir Mu'awiyah dan membujuknya untuk berbaiat kepadanya. Namun pembicaraan tersebut tidak membuahkan hasil; setelah berdiskusi dengan para sahabatnya, Amir Mu'awiyah menolak menerima 'Ali sebagai Khalifah baru. Jareer, sambil menginformasikan kepada 'Ali tentang penolakan Amir Mu'awiyah untuk menerimanya, menunjukkan bahwa ia juga telah membuat persiapan pertahanan militer. Sebagian sahabat menyatakan ketidakpuasan atas hal ini. Ketika Jareer bin 'Abdullah melihat sikap seperti itu, ia sangat kecewa dan bersedih. Ia berpendapat lebih baik tidak terlibat dan pindah ke Kufah bersama keluarganya. Ia menghabiskan sisa hidupnya dalam ketenangan di Qarqisiya dan tidak berpartisipasi dalam Perang Jamal maupun Shiffin.

Pada tahun 54 Hijriah beliau wafat di tempat tinggalnya dan pergi menuju Surga, dalam keadaan damai dengan dirinya. Seratus hadith terhubung dengannya. Dari jumlah tersebut, delapan di antaranya disepakati secara bulat oleh para ulama terkemuka.

Keindahan lahiriah dan keindahan batin akhlak Jareer berada dalam keharmonisan dan keselarasan sempurna. Ketika rambutnya memutih, beliau mewarnainya dengan henna, dan tampak semakin mengesankan. Semua orang yang melihatnya mengagumi kepribadiannya yang berwibawa.

"Kembalilah kepada Tuhanmu dengan hati yang ridha lagi diridhai-Nya!" (QS. Al-Fajr: 28)

Ja'far bin Abi Talib — Suatu ketika Jibril berkata kepada Nabi

"Setelah beliau syahid, Allah memberikannya dua sayap menggantikan kedua lengannya yang telah terputus; beliau dapat terbang ke mana pun yang dikehendakinya bersama

para malaikat."

Ja'far bin Abi Talib

Beliau adalah sepupu Nabi dan saudara kandung 'Ali. Beliau sepuluh tahun lebih tua darinya. Beliau selalu siap membantu orang miskin, anak yatim, dan mereka yang membutuhkan. Beliau adalah seorang Muslim yang terkemuka dan sejati, yang merupakan perwujudan dari kemurahan hati dan kedermawanan. Beliau juga memiliki keistimewaan berhijrah dua kali, sekali ke Ethiopia dan sekali ke Al-Madinah. Ketika kaum Muslimin berhijrah ke Ethiopia, Nabi menunjuknya sebagai pemimpin rombongan.

Beliau adalah Mujahid yang menikmati terbang bersama para malaikat di Surga dengan dua sayap yang diberikan Allah kepadanya sebagai pengganti lengan yang hilang dalam jihad.

Bacaan Al-Quran beliau begitu merdu sehingga menggetarkan jiwa para pendengarnya. Beliau adalah orator populer yang kefasihan dan penguasaannya atas bahasa Arab serta bantahan-bantahannya yang berani dan tanggapan-tanggapannya yang cepat dalam debat membuat takjub bahkan politisi selihai dan seterkenal 'Amr bin 'Aas.

Nabi mengatakan kepada Ja'far bin Abi Talib bahwa ia menyerupai beliau dalam penampilan dan akhlak, dan ia sangat gembira mendengar hal ini. Nabi juga memberikannya gelar 'Makhluk Bersayap', 'Dua Sayap', dan 'Bapak Orang Miskin.' Beliau juga mengangkat derajatnya dengan mencium dahinya. Ketika Nabi mendengar berita kesyahidannya, beliau bersedih dan air mata mengalir di pipinya. Saat itulah Jibril menyampaikan kabar gembira kepada Nabi tentang terbangnya Ja'far bin Abi Talib bersama para malaikat di Surga dengan penuh kegembiraan. Ketika bulbul istana Nabi, Hassan bin Thabit, mendengar berita kesyahidannya, ia mengungkapkan cintanya kepada keluarga Nabi dengan secara spontan membacakan syair untuk menghormatinya.

@ Abu Bakr Siddiq

@ 'Ali bin Abi Talib

@ Zaid bin Harithah — 'Uthman bin 'Affan

Zubair bin Awam — 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf — Sa'd bin Abi Waqas

Talhah bin 'Ubaidullah — Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah — Abu Salamah

Arqam bin Abi Arqam — 'Uthman bin Madh'oon bin Habib — Qadamah bin Madh'oon — 'Abdullah bin Madh'oon — 'Ubaidah bin Harith

Sa'eed bin Zaid

Fatimah bint Khattab, Asma' bint Abi Bakr, Khabab bin Aratt

@ 'Umair bin Abi Waqas

@ 'Abdullah bin Mas'ood — @ Mas'ood Qari

@ Saleet bin 'Amr

@ 'Ayash bin Abi Rabee'ah — @ Asma' bint Salamah — @ Khanees bin Hathafah

@ 'Amer bin Rabee'ah

@ 'Abdullah bin Jahash

@ Abu Ahmad bin Jahash

Dan setelah mereka, Ja'far bin Abi Talib memasuki agama Islam. Beliau memiliki tiga keistimewaan. Beliau adalah anggota keluarga Nabi. Beliau adalah anggota dari kelompok istimewa orang-orang yang pertama kali menerima Islam. Dan beliau mengorbankan hidupnya di jalan Allah. Ada sebuah riwayat yang dinisbatkan kepada 'Abdullah bin Ja'far, Abu Musa Ash'ari, dan 'Amr bin 'Aas bahwa Nabi biasa memanggil Ja'far bin Abi Talib 'Abu Al-Masakin' (Bapak Orang Miskin).

Ada empat bersaudara dalam keluarga tersebut dan terdapat selisih sepuluh tahun di antara masing-masing mereka. Talib bin Abi Talib adalah yang tertua. Berikutnya adalah 'Aqeel, kemudian Ja'far. Dan yang termuda adalah 'Ali bin Abi Talib. Abu Hurairah mengutip Nabi bersabda bahwa beliau melihat Ja'far bin Abi Talib terbang di Surga bersama para malaikat. Beliau juga berkata:

"Setiap kali kami merasa sangat lapar, Ja'far akan membawa kami ke rumahnya dan menghidangkan apa pun yang kebetulan ada di rumahnya. Beliau sangat senang memberi makan orang miskin dan yang membutuhkan serta melayani mereka."

"Ja'far adalah anggota keluarganya dan menyerupai beliau dalam penampilan dan akhlak."

'Abdullah bin Mas'ood dan Ummul Mu'minin Umm Salamah meriwayatkan bahwa rombongan kedua yang berhijrah ke Ethiopia terdiri dari delapan puluh tiga orang. Di antara para emigran yang tertindas ini terdapat para

Sahabat terkemuka seperti Ja'far bin Abi Talib, Abu Musa Ash'ari, 'Abdullah bin Arfah, dan 'Uthman bin Madh'oon bin Habib.

Umm Salamah menulis bahwa ketika kaum Quraisy mengetahui bahwa rombongan migran ini menjalani kehidupan yang damai dan tenteram di Ethiopia, mereka murka. Merencanakan untuk membakar amarah Kaisar Ethiopia Najashi terhadap para Muhajirin ini dan mengusir mereka, mereka mengirim negarawan paling licik mereka, 'Amr bin 'Aas dan 'Abdullah bin Abi Rabee'ah dengan banyak hadiah mahal ke istana Kaisar Najashi. Mereka juga diinstruksikan untuk menemui para pejabat istana, menteri, bangsawan, dan pemimpin agama, serta memberikan hadiah kepada mereka untuk menarik mereka ke sudut pandang mereka. Kedua orang tersebut melakukan seperti yang diperintahkan. Ketika mereka merasa yakin bahwa persiapan awal telah berjalan lancar dan terarah, serta para tokoh penting kerajaan akan membantu mereka, mereka memutuskan untuk menghadap di istana.

Pada suatu hari yang cerah, dengan perasaan sangat puas dan yakin bahwa tujuan mereka sudah dalam jangkauan, mereka pergi menemui Kaisar Najashi. Najashi duduk di singgasananya dalam kemegahan kerajaan penuh dan semua bangsawan berkumpul di istana. 'Amr bin 'Aas dan 'Abdullah bin Abi Rabee'ah memberi salam dengan bersujud di lantai di hadapannya. Senang dengan mereka, ia mempersilakan mereka duduk di kedua sisinya. Dengan sangat rendah hati mereka berkata:

"Yang Mulia, rakyat kami telah mengirimkan hadiah-hadiah ini untuk Anda, kiranya

berkenanlah menerima persembahan kami."

"Kaisar Najashi mengangguk dan hadiah-hadiah tersebut diterima. Menganggap ini sebagai momen yang tepat, mereka kembali membungkuk dalam-dalam dan berkata, "Kami sangat menghormati Anda; bahkan karena kecintaan kami yang mendalam kepada Anda dan negeri Anda, kami memberanikan diri untuk memperingatkan Anda bahwa beberapa orang pembuat masalah dari negeri kami telah berlindung di tanah Anda yang damai. Mereka telah berkhianat terhadap agama nenek moyang mereka dan melarikan diri dari negeri kami ke sini. Karena tindakan subversif mereka inilah saat ini saudara berbalik melawan saudara dan terdapat perpecahan serta perselisihan di antara anggota keluarga yang sama. Nasihat tulus kami adalah agar
